NAKSHATRA JAYESTHA
THE CHIEF STAR. THE SENIORMOST.MOST EXCELLENT.PRE‐EMINENT.
Anthropomorphically Middle Finger and Neck of Kalapurush. Jyeshtha
means the eldest one, the senior roost, the chief one, more excellent than,
preferred one or someone or something supreme glorious.
In mythology there are many stories about Indra and his drink Soma. Indra is
personified. His companion is Maarut and has formed relations with Asvins.
He has unlimited power, and he is governed by himself and exists from time
immemorial. He is termed Satakratu, — possessing a hundred powers. It is
easy to understand the allegories if Indra is substituted for Indriyas (senses)
and Soma for mind. However, when affected badly it gives dire indigence and
poverty.
Jyeshtha born have excellent physical stamina and a good physical
appearance.The qualities make them appear like a very proud person, but the
facts are actually different.
